name: Senomorphics Coverage Assessment for June 13, 2026

assessment_window.basis: Backfill assessment of the published June 1 senomorphics baseline plus the June 7 field-change update; no new external source search was run for this assessment.

summary: Coverage is adequate for the current low-confidence human_biomarker_signal public claim because the local map contains two direct but small human senescence/SASP biomarker anchors, a short-term oral rapamycin human safety and no-functional-change boundary, aged-mouse functional and lifespan or healthspan signals, coherent mTOR, NF-kB, JAK, and calcium-homeostasis SASP mechanisms, and a June 7 surveillance pass that added a material mianserin preclinical source while refreshing registry context. Coverage is not strong because treated-human evidence remains local, short, or acute-stress focused; candidate interventions are pleiotropic and overlap nutrient-sensing, inflammatory, serotonin-receptor, and calcium-homeostasis biology; no human trial connects senescence/SASP biomarker movement to durable function, tissue resilience, disease modification, mortality, or LEV-relevant outcomes; chronic SASP suppression may interfere with repair, immune surveillance, or tumor suppression; active registries have no posted senomorphic efficacy results; and recent-review, observational, and formal seminal-anchor triangulation has not been recorded. Ordinary surveillance can continue.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline includes the direct human anchors for the public stage: topical rapamycin reduced skin p16INK4A and increased collagen VII in an exploratory placebo-controlled skin-aging study, and metformin lowered skeletal-muscle senescence/SASP and fibrosis-linked markers in an older-adult bed-rest and re-ambulation model. A short-term oral rapamycin older-adult pilot adds feasibility and no-functional-change boundary evidence.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Human coverage is adequate only for biomarker-stage interpretation; it does not show durable function, tissue resilience, disease modification, mortality, or systemic rejuvenation.

evidence_categories.rationale: The assessed artifacts do not systematically map observational cohorts, natural experiments, real-world drug exposure, longitudinal SASP biomarkers, or tissue-specific senescence-marker trajectories. The June 7 surveillance pass noted a three-person observational GLP-1 skin p21 pilot as method-limited context, but it was not staged.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Future source-completeness work could decide whether observational metformin, rapalog, GLP-1, JAK, calcium-homeostasis, or senescence-biomarker records help bound attribution and safety.

evidence_categories.rationale: The map contains aged-mouse functional support for JAK inhibition and a post-baseline mianserin/Ca2+ source reporting improved aging phenotypes and lifespan extension in progeroid and naturally aged mice, with human aging/progeria-derived cell senescence support.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Animal functional and lifespan evidence does not upgrade the treated-human stage, and translation remains constrained by intervention-specific safety and attribution.

evidence_categories.rationale: Mechanistic coverage spans metformin IKK/NF-kB-linked SASP suppression, rapamycin-sensitive mTOR/IL1A and mTOR/MAPKAPK2/ZFP36L1 SASP regulation, JAK inhibition in senescent human cells and aged mice, and mianserin-linked calcium-homeostasis/SASP biology in human-derived cells and mouse models.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Mechanistic coherence does not prove that clinical effects are specifically senomorphic rather than general anti-inflammatory, metabolic, mTOR, serotonin-receptor, calcium-homeostasis, or stress-response effects.

evidence_categories.rationale: The outlook and reviews preserve the main limits: topical rapamycin is local skin evidence, metformin is an acute disuse/recovery biomarker study, JAK and mianserin remain preclinical for aging use, and the oral rapamycin older-adult pilot did not improve measured cognitive, physical-performance, self-perceived health, or most immune parameters.
evidence_categories.gap_note: A broader failed-program, null human endpoint, or adverse-registry map has not been formally reconciled.

evidence_categories.rationale: The local map contains short-term topical rapamycin, metformin, and oral rapamycin human safety context plus explicit JAK and chronic SASP-suppression cautions, but it does not deeply map long-term preventive safety, infection risk, wound-healing effects, cancer or tumor-surveillance tradeoffs, metabolic burden, neuropsychiatric or CNS risk for mianserin-like agents, or durability of senescence-marker changes.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Do not strengthen confidence or LEV relevance without longer-duration human safety and durability data tied to tissue-relevant senescence/SASP endpoints and function.

evidence_categories.rationale: The June 7 surveillance pass refreshed the two registry-linked human evidence records: NCT03103893 remained completed with no ClinicalTrials.gov results section while the peer-reviewed topical rapamycin result is already public, and NCT03107884 remained active, not recruiting, with no posted registry results while the published metformin bed-rest substudy remains public evidence. The pass also logged no-results watch context for mTOR inhibitor, rapamycin, everolimus, sirolimus, and metformin registries.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Continue watching NCT03107884, NCT06727305, NCT07191353, NCT05237687, NCT07395960, and adjacent senescence/SASP registries for posted results or material endpoint changes.

evidence_categories.rationale: The backfill reviewed local bootstrap and surveillance artifacts. The June 7 surveillance pass found a recent review-level aging-therapeutics record as context, but no formal recent-review, guideline, meta-analysis, or comparative triangulation has been reconciled against the eight-source public map.
evidence_categories.gap_note: A future source-completeness pass should compare recent senomorphics, SASP, rapalog, metformin, JAK, calcium-homeostasis, and cellular-senescence review literature against the current source map.

evidence_categories.rationale: The current map includes plausible anchors for topical rapamycin human p16/collagen VII, metformin older-adult muscle SASP biomarkers, JAK SASP suppression and aged-mouse function, mianserin/Ca2+ human-cell and mouse healthspan/lifespan support, metformin IKK/NF-kB mechanism, two mTOR-SASP rapamycin mechanisms, and oral rapamycin older-adult feasibility and no-functional-change boundary.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Formal must-include, review-cited, guideline-cited, registry-network, or citation-network auditing could still change source-completeness confidence.

evidence_categories.rationale: The outlook, bootstrap notes, surveillance log, and reviews explicitly distinguish senomorphics from senolytics, rapalog/nutrient-sensing claims, metformin metabolic claims, JAK immunomodulation, GLP-1 skin context, dasatinib/quercetin delivery, and generic anti-inflammatory or regenerative claims. The mianserin update is kept as preclinical SASP/Ca2+ biology rather than treated-human evidence.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Boundary maintenance remains important because candidate drugs are pleiotropic and many future senescence records may belong primarily to senolytics, rapalogs, nutrient sensing, immune modulation, metabolic therapy, or disease-specific tracks.

search_log_summary: This backfill reviewed local project artifacts: the published Senomorphics outlook, June 1 bootstrap session, published baseline candidate bundle, source-fidelity and interpretation reviews, baseline publication event, June 7 field-change session, published surveillance candidate bundle, surveillance evidence reviews, and June 7 publication event. The surveillance log included PubMed, ClinicalTrials.gov, trial-audit, and context-source checks through June 7. No new PubMed, registry, preprint, citation-network, or web source search was run for this assessment.
source_selection_notes: PMID 31761958 and PMID 37486024 are the direct human biomarker-stage anchors for topical rapamycin skin p16/collagen VII and metformin older-adult muscle senescence/SASP markers.
PMID 29408453 provides a short-term older-adult oral rapamycin feasibility and no-functional-change boundary.
PMID 26578790 and PMID 42251040 provide the main preclinical functional, healthspan, lifespan, and human-derived-cell senescence support for JAK inhibition and mianserin/Ca2+ biology.
PMID 23521863, PMID 26147250, and PMID 26280535 anchor metformin IKK/NF-kB and rapamycin-sensitive mTOR-SASP mechanisms.

The June 7 surveillance pass found no treated-human efficacy upgrade, no posted registry result from the refreshed human registries, and no correction or retraction that changed the baseline interpretation.

known_gaps.description: No treated-human senomorphic trial in the current map links senescence or SASP biomarker movement to durable function, tissue resilience, disease modification, mortality, or broad aging benefit.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep the public stage at human_biomarker_signal until randomized human trials connect tissue-relevant senescence/SASP markers to sustained functional or clinical outcomes.

known_gaps.description: Rapamycin, metformin, JAK inhibition, and mianserin each affect broad pathways beyond senomorphic SASP modulation, making causal attribution to senescence phenotype modulation uncertain.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Require endpoint designs that separate senomorphic activity from general anti-inflammatory, nutrient-sensing, metabolic, serotonin-receptor, calcium-homeostasis, or stress-response effects.

known_gaps.description: The current map does not resolve long-term safety tradeoffs from chronic SASP modulation, including immune effects, infection risk, wound repair, tumor surveillance, metabolic effects, and intervention-specific risks.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Do not strengthen confidence or assign LEV-relevant timing without longer-duration human safety, durability, and risk-benefit evidence.

known_gaps.description: The direct human biomarker evidence is limited to topical skin rapamycin and an older-adult bed-rest/recovery metformin model rather than repeated systemic aging or disease-prevention trials.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Continue presenting the current human evidence as local or acute-stress biomarker support, not systemic rejuvenation or durable functional benefit.

known_gaps.description: JAK and mianserin provide aged-mouse function, healthspan, or lifespan support, but intervention-specific feasibility, dosing, safety, and treated-human translation remain unresolved.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep animal lifespan and function signals as preclinical support until human senescence/SASP endpoints and functional outcomes are tested.

known_gaps.description: The refreshed topical rapamycin and metformin registries have no ClinicalTrials.gov results sections, and adjacent mTOR inhibitor, rapamycin, everolimus, sirolimus, and metformin registries remain no-results watch items.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Continue ordinary surveillance for NCT03107884, NCT06727305, NCT07191353, NCT05237687, NCT07395960, and other senescence/SASP registries with posted results or status changes.

known_gaps.description: Observational, natural-experiment, real-world exposure, and longitudinal senescence-marker evidence has not been systematically mapped for senomorphic candidates.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Use a future source-completeness pass to decide whether observational rapalog, metformin, GLP-1, JAK, calcium-homeostasis, or senescence-biomarker records improve context or safety boundaries.

known_gaps.description: Recent review, meta-analysis, guideline, and formal seminal-anchor triangulation across senomorphics, SASP biology, rapalogs, metformin, JAK inhibition, and calcium-homeostasis interventions has not been recorded.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Compare recent review and citation-network coverage against the current eight-source public map in a later source-completeness pass.

known_gaps.description: Senomorphics overlaps heavily with senolytics, rapalogs, nutrient sensing, immune modulation, metabolic drugs, GLP-1 skin context, and disease-specific anti-inflammatory records.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep assigning records by intended mechanism, endpoint, comparator, and aging relevance, and avoid upgrading senomorphic claims from pleiotropic drug effects alone.

next_coverage_action: Continue ordinary surveillance for treated-human senescence/SASP biomarker trials, functional or disease outcomes, registry results, chronic safety, JAK and mTOR safety tradeoffs, mianserin/Ca2+ translation, and taxonomy-boundary records. Reserve coverage repair for a later source-completeness pass focused on observational evidence, recent-review triangulation, formal seminal-anchor auditing, broader null or safety maps, and active registry network checks if no material delta appears.
